findings.title: Reverts prior preconfigured jinja2.Environment autoescape fix to deprecated env_options pattern
findings.problem: This change reverts the immediately-prior commit e11187e ('fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape'), whose commit message states the deliberate rationale: 'Starlette removed the **env_options passthrough in Jinja2Templates, breaking autoescape=True on newer versions. Pass a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead, which is the supported API.' The diff moves back to Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True), which passes autoescape through **env_options. Verified against the installed stack (starlette 0.52.1, fastapi 0.132.0) that this path is now deprecation-warned: Jinja2Templates.__init__ emits 'DeprecationWarning: Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead.' whenever any env_options kwarg is passed. So this diff intentionally undoes an established, documented fix and re-adopts the exact anti-pattern the framework and the prior commit steer away from.
findings.fix_hint: Confirm with the author whether this revert is intentional. If the goal is simply to drop the local jinja2 import, the supported equivalent is to keep the preconfigured jinja2.Environment(loader=..., autoescape=True) + Jinja2Templates(env=_jinja_env) form from e11187e (no deprecation, version-robust). If reverting is deliberate, capture why in the commit message so the next reader does not re-apply e11187e as a 'fix' and ping-pong the file.
findings.why_it_matters: The prior fix existed specifically because the kwarg passthrough was slated for removal on newer Starlette. On a version where the passthrough is fully removed (not just deprecated), autoescape=True stops reaching Jinja2 and autoescaping could silently fall back to the default. Here the net security posture happens to be preserved because starlette's _create_env does env_options.setdefault('autoescape', True) — the default is already ON — so on this pinned version escaping is not disabled. But the change re-introduces the fragility the previous commit removed and reintroduces a deprecation warning at import time.

findings.title: Reverts to deprecated Jinja2Templates(**env_options) form — emits DeprecationWarning on starlette 0.52.1
findings.problem: This diff reverts the immediately-preceding commit e11187e ('fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape'), switching from Jinja2Templates(env=_jinja_env) back to Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True). The installed starlette 0.52.1 templating.py Jinja2Templates.__init__ now warns 'DeprecationWarning: Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead.' whenever any **env_options (like autoescape) are passed alongside directory. Confirmed at runtime: passing autoescape=True raises the DeprecationWarning (and under -W error::DeprecationWarning it becomes a hard failure). Functionally autoescape is still True (verified env.autoescape == True), so this is NOT an XSS regression — but it re-introduces a deprecation the previous commit was explicitly written to remove, and autoescape is redundant since starlette's _create_env already does env_options.setdefault('autoescape', True).
findings.fix_hint: Keep the preconfigured jinja2.Environment from commit e11187e, or at minimum drop the redundant autoescape=True kwarg (starlette defaults it to True) to avoid the DeprecationWarning; if the kwarg form is intentional, add a filterwarnings note so a future -W error in CI doesn't break the app at import time.
findings.why_it_matters: The kwarg form is deprecated in starlette 0.52.1 and slated for removal; when starlette drops **env_options support this line becomes a hard TypeError at module import, taking down every web route (dashboard, config, search-now, all htmx partials — routes.py is imported by __main__.py and scheduler.py, so app startup fails entirely). Today it is only a warning, hence low severity, but it re-opens tech debt the prior commit closed.

findings.title: Revert to deprecated Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True) pattern undoes prior autoescape fix commit
findings.problem: CLAUDE.md (Security, absolute): 'in Jinja2, never apply | safe to user content or disable autoescaping.' This diff reverts commit e11187e ('fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape'), whose message states 'Starlette removed the **env_options passthrough in Jinja2Templates, breaking autoescape=True on newer versions. Pass a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead, which is the supported API.' The diff reintroduces the exact Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True) call shape that commit deliberately replaced. Verified against the project's actually-installed starlette==0.52.1: Jinja2Templates.__init__ still forwards **env_options to _create_env, which does env_options.setdefault('autoescape', True), so autoescape is in fact still True today — this is not a live violation right now. However, starlette 0.52.1 emits 'DeprecationWarning: Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead' on exactly this call, confirming the passthrough is being phased out, consistent with what the prior fix commit's message already described happening.
findings.fix_hint: Restore the preconfigured jinja2.Environment approach from commit e11187e: build jinja2.Environment(loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(str(TEMPLATES_DIR)), autoescape=True) and pass it via Jinja2Templates(env=_jinja_env), which is the non-deprecated, version-stable API and avoids relying on env_options passthrough that Starlette is deprecating.
findings.why_it_matters: If a future starlette upgrade removes the deprecated **env_options passthrough entirely (which the emitted DeprecationWarning signals is planned, and which the reverted commit's own message says already happened once), autoescape=True would silently stop being applied. Jinja2's Environment defaults to autoescape=False, so templates rendering any user-controlled data (instance names, config values, etc.) would emit unescaped HTML with no error or test failure to catch it, opening an XSS vector.

findings.title: Revert of prior autoescape-regression fix reintroduces fragile Jinja2Templates construction pattern
findings.problem: This diff reverts commit e11187e ('fix: use preconfigured jinja2.Environment for autoescape'), whose commit message states Starlette previously removed/broke the **env_options passthrough in Jinja2Templates, silently dropping autoescape=True. Reading the currently-installed starlette==0.52.1 / fastapi==0.133.0 source confirms Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True) still works today (the passthrough is honored in _create_env), but that same code path already emits 'DeprecationWarning: Extra environment options are deprecated. Use a preconfigured jinja2.Environment instead.' for exactly this call — signaling this pattern is a known regression risk on a future Starlette release. fastapi/jinja2 are unpinned (no version floor) in pyproject.toml, and there is no test asserting templates.env.autoescape is True, so nothing in CI would catch autoescape silently being disabled again.
findings.fix_hint: Restore the preconfigured jinja2.Environment pattern from e11187e (construct jinja2.Environment(loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(str(TEMPLATES_DIR)), autoescape=True) and pass it via Jinja2Templates(env=_jinja_env)), or at minimum add a regression test asserting templates.env.autoescape is True and pin a starlette/fastapi floor so a future upgrade can't silently drop autoescape.
findings.why_it_matters: If a future dependency upgrade reintroduces the Starlette regression this revert undoes, Jinja2 autoescape silently stops applying, and any user-controlled value rendered into a template (instance names, config values, search-log entries) is emitted unescaped — reflected/stored XSS in the dashboard — with no test to catch it before it ships.

findings.title: Template initialization passes an unsupported keyword and breaks startup
findings.problem: Line 45 now calls Jinja2Templates(directory=..., autoescape=True), but the installed FastAPI/Starlette Jinja2Templates.__init__ accepts only directory, context_processors, and env. I verified in .venv that constructing it with autoescape=True raises TypeError. Because triggarr/__main__.py imports router from this module, the app can fail during import before any route serves.
findings.fix_hint: Restore the explicit jinja2.Environment(..., autoescape=True) passed via Jinja2Templates(env=...), or use Jinja2Templates(directory=...) without unsupported kwargs and add an import/startup test under the locked dependency set.
findings.why_it_matters: Because triggarr/__main__.py imports router from this module, a constructor TypeError at module import would take the app down before any route serves.
